name: Digest long emails with MySQL and Microsoft Teams

parameters.message: =✅ Digest long emails with MySQL and Microsoft Teams — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### Receive Inbound Webhook

Entry point: listens for POST calls on `/wf05261-draft`. Use the TEST url while building, switch callers to the PRODUCTION url after publishing.

Next: Read Recent from MySQL

### Read Recent from MySQL

Selects rows from the MySQL table (`returnAll`) — the read side of the store.

Ports: ①→ Newest First · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 MySQL connection

### Newest First

Orders items (newest first) so limits and digests pick the most relevant records.

Next: Trim to Digest Size

### Trim to Digest Size

Caps the stream at 15 items to protect downstream quotas.

Next: Summarize Content (AI)

### Summarize Content (AI)

Map-reduce summarization chain — handles long content without blowing the context window.

Ports: ①→ Post Digest to Microsoft Teams, Post Digest to Slack · ②→ Capture Failure Context

⚠ failures exit via the error rail

### Groq Chat Model

Chat model powering the AI step (model: `llama-3.3-70b-versatile`). Swap providers here without rewiring.

Feeds **Summarize Content (AI)** as `languageModel`

🔑 Groq API key

### Post Digest to Microsoft Teams

Posts a chat message into a Microsoft Teams channel — same role as a Slack notify, for teams standardized on Teams.

Ports: ①→ Confirm All Sends · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

nodes.name: Note: Post Digest to Microsoft Teams

parameters.content:
### Post Digest to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#content`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

Ports: ①→ Confirm All Sends · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Confirm All Sends

Rejoins 2 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Archive Digest in MySQL

### Archive Digest in MySQL

Inserts a row into the MySQL table using fields auto-mapped from the incoming item.

Ports: ①→ Sort Drafts Newest First · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 MySQL connection

### Sort Drafts Newest First

Orders items (newest first) so limits and digests pick the most relevant records.

Next: Align Field Names

### Align Field Names

Normalizes field names so every source feeds the same downstream schema.

Next: Cap Batch Volume

### Cap Batch Volume

Caps the stream at 50 items to protect downstream quotas.

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (MySQL)

### Append Audit Row (MySQL)

Inserts a row into the MySQL table using fields auto-mapped from the incoming item.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Microsoft Teams · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 MySQL connection

### Send Summary to Microsoft Teams

Posts a chat message into a Microsoft Teams channel — same role as a Slack notify, for teams standardized on Teams.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

nodes.name: Note: Send Summary to Microsoft Teams

parameters.content:
### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Microsoft Teams

### Escalate Failure to Microsoft Teams

Posts a chat message into a Microsoft Teams channel — same role as a Slack notify, for teams standardized on Teams.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

nodes.name: Note: Escalate Failure to Microsoft Teams

parameters.content:
# Digest long emails with MySQL and Microsoft Teams

**Pattern:** AI Summarize & Route · **Level:** Advanced · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Digest long emails with MySQL and Microsoft Teams. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual social copy is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Receive Inbound Webhook → Read Recent from MySQL → Newest First → Trim to Digest Size → Summarize Content (AI) → Post Digest to Microsoft Teams → Confirm All Sends → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to ai summarize & route without manual effort
- Anyone automating social copy in content creation
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Standardizes the process every time
- Runs 24/7 in the background
- Keeps your systems in sync

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Groq API key; Microsoft OAuth2; MySQL connection; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Summarization uses a Chain, not an Agent, because the step is fixed — there's no decision about which tool to call, just 'summarize this text.' Posting to multiple channels runs as parallel branches, not sequential sends, so a slow API on one channel never delays the message that was already ready on another.

## Practice exercises

You've got the basics covered — push further:

1. Add a third notification channel and watch the Merge node's input count update.
2. Change 'Trim to Digest Size' from 15 to 5 and see how it narrows the summary's scope.
3. Swap the Summarization Chain for a Q&A Chain to answer questions instead of just recapping.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🔐 Security

Never trust inbound payloads: the webhook path is unguessable. Add header auth or IP allow-listing for defense in depth. Secrets live in n8n credentials — never in node parameters.

## 🤖 AI cluster

Purple nodes form one AI unit: the root node orchestrates, sub-nodes plug in via typed ports (model / memory / tools / parser). Swap the chat model without rewiring anything else. Costs scale with tokens — keep prompts lean and cap iterations.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your social copy process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
